response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and relevant experience with distributed systems (Dunnhumby's millions-of-requests microservices architecture) and cloud infrastructure (Optilyz AWS CDK work), he critically fails to meet a core minimum qualification: 8 years of C++ programming experience. His resume shows only approximately one year of documented C++ work at National Instruments (2014-2015). Though his ~5 years of large-scale infrastructure experience across multiple roles approaches the stated requirement, the severe C++ gap makes him unsuitable for a technical lead role on a cloud compute platform requiring deep C++ expertise.
strengths: Proven ability to own and ship complex projects from conception through production across diverse technical domains
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals including TDD, CI/CD, architectural thinking, and mentoring others through workshops and code kata sessions
Directly relevant distributed systems experience: handled microservices with millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby with low-latency optimization
concerns: Only ~1 year of documented C++ programming experience versus the 8-year minimum requirement
Recent experience (Optilyz, re:cinq) focused on full-stack application and DevTools development rather than infrastructure platform work
No evidence of formal technical leadership or mentoring in cloud/DevOps engineering roles specifically
